name: Document Scanning (VisionKit)
promptInjection:
CAPABILITY: Document Scanning (VisionKit)

APPROACH:
- Use VNDocumentCameraViewController (VisionKit) for the system document scanning UI.
- This is a UIKit view controller — wrap it with UIViewControllerRepresentable for SwiftUI.
- The system handles edge detection, perspective correction, and image enhancement automatically.
- Returns VNDocumentCameraScan with one or more page images.

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Create a struct conforming to UIViewControllerRepresentable.
2. In makeUIViewController, return VNDocumentCameraViewController() with delegate set to coordinator.
3. Coordinator implements VNDocumentCameraViewControllerDelegate with three methods:
   - documentCameraViewController(_:didFinishWith scan: VNDocumentCameraScan) — success, get pages.
   - documentCameraViewControllerDidCancel(_:) — user cancelled.
   - documentCameraViewController(_:didFailWithError:) — error occurred.
4. Access pages: scan.pageCount, scan.imageOfPage(at: index) returns UIImage.

SAVING SCANNED PAGES:
- Save UIImages as JPEG/PNG to the documents directory.
- For PDF generation: use UIGraphicsPDFRenderer to create a PDF from the scanned page images.
- Store metadata (title, date, page count, file paths) with Codable + UserDefaults.

PDF GENERATION (common companion task):
  let renderer = UIGraphicsPDFRenderer(bounds: CGRect(origin: .zero, size: pageSize))
  let data = renderer.pdfData { ctx in
      for i in 0..<scan.pageCount {
          let image = scan.imageOfPage(at: i)
          ctx.beginPage()
          image.draw(in: CGRect(origin: .zero, size: pageSize))
      }
  }
  try data.write(to: pdfURL)

OCR PAIRING:
- After scanning, optionally run VNRecognizeTextRequest on each page image to extract text.
- This combines document-scanning (VisionKit) with text-recognition (Vision) for a full scan-to-text flow.

PRESENTATION:
- Present via .sheet(isPresented:) { DocumentScannerView(...) }
- The scanner UI is full-screen and managed by the system.

DEVICE REQUIREMENT:
- VNDocumentCameraViewController requires a real device with a camera.
- Check VNDocumentCameraViewController.isSupported before presenting.
- Show a clear fallback UI on Simulator or unsupported devices.

ANTI-PATTERNS:
- Do NOT try to build a custom document scanner with AVFoundation — use VisionKit's built-in UI.
- Do NOT forget to dismiss the scanner in the delegate methods.
- Do NOT assume scan.pageCount is always 1 — users can scan multiple pages.
- Do NOT store raw UIImages in memory for many pages — save to disk and load on demand.
- Do NOT import VisionKit AND try to use DataScannerViewController for document scanning — they are different APIs.
antiPatterns: Building a custom document scanner with AVFoundation instead of using VisionKit
Not dismissing the scanner in delegate methods
Assuming scan always has exactly 1 page
Holding many full-resolution UIImages in memory (save to disk instead)
